req.title: Create encounter (audit)
req.statement: A clinician shall create an Encounter for a patient with start and end timestamps; end before start shall be rejected. An AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation and tested end-to-end.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates: (1) a clinician can create an Encounter with valid start/end timestamps, (2) end-before-start must be rejected with HTTP 422, (3) an AuditEvent must be emitted for every operation. I apply EP for the happy-path valid encounter, NEG for the invalid temporal ordering, BVA for the equal start==end boundary, STC to verify the state transition from no-encounter to stored-encounter, and DT to cover the audit-event emission rule across both success and failure paths. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that provision and teardown state.

tests.name: test_create_encounter_valid_timestamps
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician and a valid bearer token
System is able to emit AuditEvents
actions: Poll AuditEvent endpoint filtering by encounter id
expected: Stored encounter start and end match submitted values
At least one AuditEvent references the created encounter
trace: R-HLT-081: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
def test_create_encounter_valid_timestamps(clinician_token, existing_patient_id, fhir_client, audit_poller):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
            "end": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
        }
    }
    resp = fhir_client.post("/Encounter", json=payload, token=clinician_token)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Encounter"
    enc_id = body["id"]
    assert body["period"]["start"] == "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z"
    assert body["period"]["end"] == "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
    audit = audit_poller.find_for_entity(f"Encounter/{enc_id}")
    assert audit is not None, "AuditEvent must be emitted for create operation"
    assert audit["action"] in ("C", "create")
    fhir_client.delete(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}", token=clinician_token)

tests.name: test_create_encounter_end_before_start_rejected
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician and a valid bearer token
actions: POST /Encounter with start='2024-06-01T10:00:00Z' and end='2024-06-01T09:00:00Z' (end < start)
Capture HTTP status and error body
Poll AuditEvent endpoint for the rejected attempt
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or equivalent error descriptor
No Encounter resource is persisted in the store
An AuditEvent with outcome=failure is emitted for the rejected operation
trace: R-HLT-081: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
def test_create_encounter_end_before_start_rejected(clinician_token, existing_patient_id, fhir_client, audit_poller):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": "2024-06-01T10:00:00Z",
            "end": "2024-06-01T09:00:00Z"
        }
    }
    resp = fhir_client.post("/Encounter", json=payload, token=clinician_token)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert "resourceType" in body and body["resourceType"] in ("OperationOutcome", "error")
    search = fhir_client.get(
        "/Encounter",
        params={"subject": existing_patient_id, "date": "2024-06-01"},
        token=clinician_token
    )
    entries = search.json().get("entry", [])
    assert not any(
        e["resource"]["period"]["start"] == "2024-06-01T10:00:00Z" for e in entries
    ), "No encounter must be stored on invalid input"
    audit = audit_poller.find_failed_for_subject(existing_patient_id, action="create")
    assert audit is not None, "AuditEvent with failure outcome must be emitted"

tests.name: test_create_encounter_equal_start_end_boundary
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician and a valid bearer token
actions: POST /Encounter with start='2024-06-01T08:00:00Z' and end='2024-06-01T08:00:00Z' (start == end, zero-duration boundary)
Poll AuditEvent endpoint for the resulting operation
expected: HTTP 201 Created (equal timestamps represent a zero-duration encounter which is valid)
Response body contains resourceType='Encounter' with matching period
An AuditEvent is emitted referencing the created encounter
trace: R-HLT-081: boundary between valid and invalid temporal ordering (start == end)
R-HLT-081: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
def test_create_encounter_equal_start_end_boundary(clinician_token, existing_patient_id, fhir_client, audit_poller):
    ts = "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z"
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {"start": ts, "end": ts}
    }
    resp = fhir_client.post("/Encounter", json=payload, token=clinician_token)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, (
        f"Zero-duration encounter should be accepted; got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["resourceType"] == "Encounter"
    enc_id = body["id"]
    assert body["period"]["start"] == ts
    assert body["period"]["end"] == ts
    audit = audit_poller.find_for_entity(f"Encounter/{enc_id}")
    assert audit is not None, "AuditEvent must be emitted for boundary create operation"
    fhir_client.delete(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}", token=clinician_token)

tests.name: test_encounter_state_transition_created_to_stored
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician and a valid bearer token
No prior Encounter exists for this patient in the test window
actions: Verify no Encounter exists for the patient (initial state: absent)
POST /Encounter with valid start and end timestamps
Verify AuditEvent chain covers both creation and read
expected: Before POST: GET returns empty bundle or 404
After POST: HTTP 201 and encounter retrievable via GET with HTTP 200
Encounter status field reflects stored state
AuditEvents present for both the create and the subsequent read
trace: R-HLT-081: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
tests.executable:
def test_encounter_state_transition_created_to_stored(clinician_token, existing_patient_id, fhir_client, audit_poller):
    pre = fhir_client.get(
        "/Encounter",
        params={"subject": existing_patient_id, "date": "2024-07-15"},
        token=clinician_token
    )
    assert pre.json().get("total", 0) == 0, "Precondition: no prior encounter"
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {
            "start": "2024-07-15T09:00:00Z",
            "end": "2024-07-15T10:00:00Z"
        }
    }
    create_resp = fhir_client.post("/Encounter", json=payload, token=clinician_token)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    enc_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    get_resp = fhir_client.get(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}", token=clinician_token)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json()["status"] == "finished"
    audit_create = audit_poller.find_for_entity(f"Encounter/{enc_id}", action="create")
    audit_read = audit_poller.find_for_entity(f"Encounter/{enc_id}", action="read")
    assert audit_create is not None
    assert audit_read is not None
    fhir_client.delete(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}", token=clinician_token)

tests.name: test_encounter_decision_table_audit_on_success_and_failure
preconditions: Caller has role:clinician and a valid bearer token
actions: POST /Encounter with valid timestamps (rule: valid period -> 201 + success audit)
POST /Encounter with end before start (rule: invalid period -> 422 + failure audit)
expected: Valid request: HTTP 201 and AuditEvent outcome=0 (success)
Invalid request: HTTP 422 and AuditEvent outcome=8 (serious failure) or equivalent failure code
trace: R-HLT-081: AuditEvent emitted for every operation
Decision table row 1: valid period -> stored + audit success
Decision table row 2: invalid period -> rejected + audit failure
tests.executable:
def test_encounter_decision_table_audit_on_success_and_failure(clinician_token, existing_patient_id, fhir_client, audit_poller):
    valid_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {"start": "2024-08-01T07:00:00Z", "end": "2024-08-01T08:00:00Z"}
    }
    r1 = fhir_client.post("/Encounter", json=valid_payload, token=clinician_token)
    assert r1.status_code == 201
    enc_id = r1.json()["id"]
    audit_ok = audit_poller.find_for_entity(f"Encounter/{enc_id}", action="create")
    assert audit_ok is not None
    assert str(audit_ok.get("outcome", "0")) == "0", "Success AuditEvent outcome must be 0"
    invalid_payload = {
        "resourceType": "Encounter",
        "status": "finished",
        "subject": {"reference": f"Patient/{existing_patient_id}"},
        "period": {"start": "2024-08-01T09:00:00Z", "end": "2024-08-01T08:00:00Z"}
    }
    r2 = fhir_client.post("/Encounter", json=invalid_payload, token=clinician_token)
    assert r2.status_code == 422
    audit_fail = audit_poller.find_failed_for_subject(existing_patient_id, action="create")
    assert audit_fail is not None
    assert str(audit_fail.get("outcome", "")) != "0", "Failure AuditEvent outcome must not be 0"
    fhir_client.delete(f"/Encounter/{enc_id}", token=clinician_token)
